combined_cases.ai_summary: Single mother with two children served eviction notice due to late rent requiring urgent support. Imminent eviction threat with children involved. Caseworker has made contact and recommended approval for imminent eviction assistance.
Client seeking assistance to settle late husband's outstanding debt. Debt settlement request without immediate crisis timeframes. Caseworker has made contact and assessment is in progress.

Family violence survivor who has been moving house to house, now facing immediate homelessness after uncle left property over a month ago. Active housing crisis requiring urgent intervention. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Client abandoned by husband following family violence with daughter. Historical DV situation without current immediate danger specified. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Single parent with 9-month-old son facing rent arrears and close to eviction, unable to afford food and basic necessities. Imminent housing threat with child in crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Client with mental health issues unable to find employment to support family financially. General hardship situation without immediate crisis timeframes. No caseworker interaction recorded.

ai_analysis.quality_summary: Significant priority assignment issues identified. Multiple cases with clear urgency indicators (eviction notices, homelessness, children in crisis) remain unassigned or incorrectly prioritized. Most cases lack caseworker interaction despite being in intake for extended periods.

flags.reason: Active homelessness post-hospital discharge with ongoing health issues requires immediate accommodation assistance
Family violence victim facing immediate homelessness after uncle's departure requires urgent housing
Single parent with infant facing imminent eviction and unable to provide basic necessities
Single mother with eviction notice served requires P2 priority for imminent housing threat
Single mother with four young children including toddler in extremely difficult circumstances
Recently arrived refugee single mother with four children requiring emergency relief
New parents with special needs infant facing financial hardship require elevated priority
Chronic anemia requiring urgent medical support that cannot wait for public health system
Previously homeless parent with son now needing immediate rent advance indicates housing instability
Parent unable to work due to child's hospitalization facing immediate financial crisis

patterns: High volume of unassigned cases at intake stage without caseworker interaction
Multiple cases involving children in crisis situations not receiving appropriate priority
Domestic violence cases not properly assessed for current safety threats
Medical emergency cases not prioritized appropriately
